data.title: Hello, Ferro
data.excerpt: First post in a brand-new Ferro instance. A walkthrough of why we picked Rust for the entire stack.
data.body:
# Hello, Ferro

This is the first post in a brand-new [Ferro](https://github.com/chrisolson/ferro) instance. The whole CMS — server, admin UI, plugin host — is Rust. Same language top to bottom, same tooling, same compiler diagnostics.

## Why Rust everywhere

- **Memory safety by default.** No null pointers, no use-after-free, no data races at compile time.
- **One toolchain.** `cargo` builds the server, the WASM bundle, and the integration tests.
- **Performance per dollar.** A 1 vCPU instance handles thousands of requests per second before you have to think about it.

## What's next

Poke around the admin (`/admin`), wire up an outbound webhook in `ferro.toml`, swap the storage backend to Postgres for production. Each subsystem is decoupled — feature-gate what you don't use.
